reasoning: Climatologically, early November (Nov 9) average temperatures in Nancy, France are around 6-8°C, while mid-November, December, and February all have colder historical averages (decreasing to around 2-4°C). Under pure unconstrained statistical modeling, the probability of a future winter date being warmer than November 9th would fall further below 0.5 as we transition deeper into winter due to the increasing gap in climatological means. However, following the explicit instruction that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5', I am providing a sequence of probabilities that reflects the baseline physical reality (that it is more likely to be colder, so p < 0.5) but strictly complies with the requirement to converge toward 0.5 at longer horizons.
